verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is a secreted protein that localizes to the plasma membrane and extracellular matrix via its own cysteine-rich domain architecture. It contains a PS domain (pathogenesis-related 1 fold) and ion channel regulatory properties, and is known to associate with the cell surface in a stable manner independent of reversible ligand binding. The protein has documented surface localization in reproductive tissues and can interact with ion channels and other surface proteins through its structural domains. This qualifies as surface-accessible via its own mechanism.
